codeReview.summary: The recorded handoff, transcript skeleton, and git state all support the branch-prep requirements: the worker confirmed a clean tree, verified `mission/feb91986-base`, created and switched to `mission/pr14-language-toggle`, and verified HEAD matched the mission base commit. Reviewing `git show f61c26a` also confirmed there was no product-code diff attributable to this feature, which is consistent with a branch-setup task.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `branch-prep-worker` says startup/cleanup are handled by `worker-base`, but the recorded worker session used `mission-worker-base`. The canonical bootstrap skill name should be clarified so future workers do not have to infer which startup skill applies.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/branch-prep-worker/SKILL.md:8 says startup is handled by `worker-base`; the transcript skeleton for worker session `b30682c6-4a96-459b-991b-12ecfe109d98` shows `Skill { "skill": "mission-worker-base" }` before executing the branch-prep flow.

summary: Pass. `pr14-branch-setup` achieved the requested clean branch setup from `mission/feb91986-base` with no product-code changes; only a documentation-level skill naming mismatch was observed.
